title: let, const and binding

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for let, const and binding; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: ECMAScript Language Specification; living specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to let, const and binding
sources.claim: let and const create block-scoped bindings, while const prevents rebinding but does not freeze referenced objects.

sources.locator: MDN JavaScript Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to let, const and binding
sources.claim: let and const create block-scoped bindings, while const prevents rebinding but does not freeze referenced objects.

canonicalExplanation.summary: let and const create block-scoped bindings, while const prevents rebinding but does not freeze referenced objects.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A const array can receive push because its binding stays fixed while the array object mutates. Your goal is to trace bindings through blocks and choose const unless reassignment is intended. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Scripts and console; pf state expressions; Primitive values and typeof; pf state variables. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: let and const create block-scoped bindings, while const prevents rebinding but does not freeze referenced objects. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Temporal dead zones, var function scope, global bindings, and closure capture affect visibility and lifetime. This draft therefore rejects: const makes every nested property of an object immutable.

includes: Use block-scoped declarations and distinguish binding reassignment from object mutation.
Trace bindings through blocks and choose const unless reassignment is intended.
excludes: Temporal dead zones, var function scope, global bindings, and closure capture affect visibility and lifetime.

outcomes: Trace bindings through blocks and choose const unless reassignment is intended.

examples.title: let, const and binding: concrete case
examples.situation: A const array can receive push because its binding stays fixed while the array object mutates.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A const array can receive push because its binding stays fixed while the array object mutates.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: const prevents rebinding; use freezing or immutable updates when object mutation must also be prevented.
examples.result: The result follows because const prevents rebinding; use freezing or immutable updates when object mutation must also be prevented.
